title: /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/.claude 内容を表示せよ
summary_text: 1. `High` `BLOCKED` floor is not enforced when diversity quorum collapses at runtime. [run-kernel-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/scripts/local/run-kernel-orchestration.sh#L244) computes `ok_count/fail_count/unavailable_lanes`, but never exits non-zero or emits `BLOCKED` when only one non-Codex lane survives. [test-run-kernel-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/tests/test-run-kernel-orchestration.sh#L159) currently accepts runs with `fail_count == 1`, so this regression path is unguarded. 2. `High` Multi-lane diversity is validated only as provider presence, not materially distinct lane families/roles. [run-kernel-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/scripts/local/run-kernel-orchestration.sh#L107) checks unique lane names and provider set `{codex, claude, glm}`, but does not require distinct roles/families; homogeneous reviewer lanes pass policy checks. [test-run-kernel-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/tests/test-run-kernel-orchestration.sh#L233) has no negative case for low role/family diversity. 3. `Medium` Fallback evidence recording misses normalized summary fields needed by cross-system policy (`missing_lane`, `fallback_provider`, exact single `fallback_reason`). [run-kernel-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/scripts/local/run-kernel-orchestration.sh#L247) records arrays (`fallback_lanes`, `unavailable_lanes`) but does not emit canonical top-level fallback triplet; tests only assert lane-level entries, not canonical fallback evidence completeness.
